bead.title: ddx doctor should verify running binary is in canonical install location
bead.description: install.sh installs the ddx binary to $HOME/.local/bin/ddx as the canonical location. But users can end up with multiple copies (e.g. /home/erik/bin/ddx from a stale install, /usr/local/bin/ddx, or a dev build on PATH ahead of the canonical one). This session had a real incident: /home/erik/bin/ddx was 053171bf (older) while /home/erik/.local/bin/ddx was 40f8db0d (current) — the shell picked the wrong one for hours, causing confusion about whether blocker fixes were actually active. ddx doctor should detect mismatches between the running binary's path and the canonical install location AND warn when multiple ddx copies exist on PATH with different SHAs.
bead.acceptance: ddx doctor (1) reports the absolute path of the currently-executing binary via os.Executable(); (2) checks whether that path matches $HOME/.local/bin/ddx (the install.sh canonical location); (3) walks PATH and reports every other 'ddx' found, flagging any whose SHA-256 differs from the running binary as a warning; (4) suggests 'rm <stale> && cp <current> <stale>' or 're-run install.sh' to fix. Test covers the multi-binary-on-PATH scenario by shadowing a fake ddx in a temp dir earlier on PATH.
